output.summary:
Change address field PHP output from esc_html() to wp_kses_post() so that
tags from RichText are rendered as actual line breaks instead of escaped HTML entities. The TSX editor already uses RichText which saves
on Enter — only the PHP frontend escaping needs to change. This matches the existing pattern used for hoursContent and contactContent in the same file.

fileChanges.description: Change address output escaping from esc_html() to wp_kses_post()
fileChanges.details:
Line 59: Change `` to ``. This is the same escaping function already used for hoursContent (line 76) and contactContent (line 86) in this file. wp_kses_post() allows safe HTML tags like
, , , while still stripping dangerous tags/attributes.

designDecisions.decision: Use wp_kses_post() instead of esc_html() for address field output
designDecisions.evidence: hoursContent and contactContent in the same file (FooterInfo.php lines 76, 86) already use wp_kses_post() for the same reason — they are RichText fields that may contain basic HTML. wp_kses_post() is the WordPress-standard function for safely outputting user content that may contain allowed HTML tags.

designDecisions.decision:
No TSX changes needed — RichText with allowedFormats=[] already handles
on Enter key
designDecisions.evidence:
The RichText component (FooterInfo.tsx lines 128-142) already converts Enter keypresses into
tags in the saved content. The allowedFormats=[] only hides the formatting toolbar (bold/italic/etc) but does not prevent line breaks. The saved HTML already contains
— only the PHP output was escaping it.

output.notes: Single-line fix. The address name field (line 56, esc_html) does NOT need changing — campus/location names should not contain HTML. Only the address text field needs this fix since it commonly contains multi-line addresses.
